Language Assistance Services – CDC 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
In November of 2023, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) updated its 2013 Language Access Plan in an effort to “ensure greater access to the life-saving services that HHS provides for people with Limited English Proficiency (LEP) and people with disabilities.”
Consistent with the updated HHS plan, CDC launched a 2024 plan which outlines the action steps the agency will take to meet the language access needs of the public while continuing to support communities that may be underserved and improving access to information for people with LEP and people with disabilities.
